mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in the Industrial Production Index signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth in the top quartile fails to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, liquidity abundance compresses short-term discount rates and inflates equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based demand acceleration that outpaces supply constraints, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of co-occurring instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6 percent, elevated joblessness signals insufficient aggregate demand and weak consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Top quartile month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth coincides with negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor income and weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative PAYEMS change show positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust monthly retail sales growth reflects strong consumer expenditure momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth in the top tercile fails to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate deepening supply-side contraction and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.
